overview: The Village of Weston Parks Committee approved signage for Yellow Banks kayak launch, deferred discussion on dogs at the aquatic center due to absent chair, and received updates on Kennedy Park fundraising and disc golf course improvements. The meeting included public comment opposing dogs at the pool and criticism of the 4 p.m. meeting time and Kennedy Park financial transparency.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Yellow Banks disc golf course update
Dog day at the aquatic center
Graffiti on the Oaklair River Trail
Topics for future meetings and staff remarks

discussions.body: Minutes were approved with a motion and second. No discussion was held. Motion carried unanimously with voice vote.

Director Sean Oster Bring presented a timeline document developed with Michael showing the project from the comp plan approval in November 2023 through the May 9th ribbon cutting. Committee member Scott asked clarifying questions about funding structure. Oster Bring explained the village had planned approximately $7 million of a $14.3 million total project for infrastructure, but those capital items were pulled pending the master plan development. The Friends of Kennedy Park are currently fundraising for lights on the upper field, having approximately $84-85,000 toward a $117,000 goal at the community foundation, with a planned public check presentation at their June tournament. The committee discussed that the 10-year timeline is for potential reevaluation, not a hard deadline.

discussions.item: Yellow Banks disc golf course update
discussions.body: Staff presented photos and PDF showing location of trees removed from the river and installation of wood mats over gas pipelines to protect them during equipment access. Dan explained the mats were installed to allow equipment passage over TC Energy (formerly ANR/ATC) pipelines, with a company representative present during work. The village has extra mats that may be used for future trail projects.

discussions.item: Dog day at the aquatic center
discussions.body: Discussion was deferred to next month's meeting because Committee Chair Katrina, who had requested this item, was absent due to an unforeseen issue. A resident named Andy who attended specifically to oppose dogs at the pool had his objection noted but was told the item would be discussed next month when the chair is present.

discussions.item: Graffiti on the Oaklair River Trail
discussions.body: Committee discussed challenges removing graffiti from concrete and benches on the trail. Staff explained they cannot get pressure washing equipment to the location due to steep ditches and narrow bridges that only accommodate pickup trucks. Options discussed included anti-vandalism coatings, luster seal, and potentially organizing volunteer work days. Committee member noted that people love the trail and would likely volunteer to help. No formal action was taken; staff will investigate cleaning options.

Dan Borchardt from Marathon County (2625 Northwestern Avenue) presented version 7 of proposed signage for kayak launches. Three of the eight total launches are in Village of Weston (Highway J, Ross Avenue, and Yellow Banks). Signs include QR code for river gauge, donation URL to Marathon County Parks Foundation which funded the signs through state grants. Dan noted the Highway J launch may be closed all season due to bridge replacement. Motion to approve installation of signs and forward to village board passed unanimously.

Staff raised potential discussion of food allergy awareness signage on playgrounds after receiving a request from an individual. Signs would remind people to keep food at picnic tables rather than on play equipment. Committee agreed to bring the item back for future discussion. Staff also announced the Mock Miller master plan process will begin with a kickoff meeting with Rettler, and the final bike and pedestrian plan open house is June 11th.

publicComment: Two public comments were offered. Andy (6504 [address inaudible]) spoke in person opposing dogs at the public pool, stating animals should not be allowed and belong in rivers and lakes instead. Jim Pensan (5002 [Street] Weston), speaking online under the name 'Vern M', criticized the 4 p.m. meeting time as discouraging public participation, questioned the Kennedy Park funding report showing exactly $8,700 in receipts and expenditures as 'too coincidental,' asked about a $10,000 grant and Glow event funds not appearing in receipts, and called the finances 'convoluted and misleading,' suggesting the Friends of Kennedy Park may be 'frauds' for not sharing complete financials.
actionItems: Defer dog day at aquatic center discussion to June meeting when Chair Katrina is present
Staff to investigate cleaning solutions and anti-vandalism coatings for Oaklair River Trail benches and infrastructure
Yellow Banks kayak launch sign installation approved and forwarded to village board
Bring food allergy awareness playground signage discussion to future meeting
Mock Miller master plan kickoff meeting with Rettler scheduled for next Tuesday
title: Parks Committee
